items.title: Last Week In Claude Code #3 — /btw, /voice, 1M Context, Diagrams & Charts
items.excerpt: Chat While Coding, Talk to Build, and Create Stunning Visuals
items.summary: Claude AI has introduced new features including voice interaction capabilities, expanded context windows to 1 million tokens, and enhanced visual creation tools for coding projects. The updates focus on enabling conversational coding workflows and improved diagram generation for technical documentation.
items.pullQuote: Voice interaction and million-token context windows transform coding workflows

items.title: What Wasn't Being Said - Part I
items.summary: The article explores the importance of looking beyond initial assumptions and surface-level thinking in educational and personal development contexts. It emphasizes developing critical thinking skills that question underlying premises rather than accepting conventional wisdom.
items.pullQuote: Going beyond intuition reveals hidden assumptions in learning

items.title: How a Skill Graph Can Turn AI Into Your Content Team

The approach focuses on organizing knowledge systematically to enable AI agents to automate various work tasks more effectively.
items.pullQuote: Turn one idea into many outputs using linked knowledge systems

items.title: Kagi is contemplating the removal of the assistant from its professional tier

items.pullQuote: Privacy search engine restructuring AI assistant subscription model

items.title: Nightingale – open-source karaoke app that works with any song on your computer
items.excerpt: Article URL: https://nightingale.cafe/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47422942 Points: 24 # Comments: 5
items.summary: Nightingale is a free, open-source karaoke application that can process any audio file on a user's computer to create karaoke versions. The software separates vocals from music tracks automatically without requiring specialized karaoke files.
items.pullQuote: Creates karaoke versions from any song on your computer automatically

items.title: The 250th Anniversary of Adam Smith's Revolutionary Text
items.excerpt: The Wealth of Nations is a revolutionary text
items.summary: Reich commemorates the 250th anniversary of Adam Smith's "The Wealth of Nations" and its enduring economic principles. The analysis connects Smith's foundational ideas to contemporary economic policy debates.
items.pullQuote: Smith's revolutionary economic theories remain relevant after 250 years

items.title: Support for Israel Has Collapsed Among Young Americans
items.excerpt: Sam Stein and Rachel Janfaza take on a striking shift among young voters: support for Israel has collapsed, with just 13% of Gen Z viewing it positively.
items.summary: Polling data reveals only 13% of Generation Z Americans view Israel positively, marking a dramatic generational shift. The analysis examines factors driving this collapse in support among younger voters.
items.pullQuote: Only 13% of Gen Z Americans view Israel positively

items.title: What Pete Hegseth's Spiritual Mentor Wants for America
items.excerpt: Pastor-theologian Douglas Wilson advocates theocracy, the restriction of the franchise from women and nonbelievers, and much else—and he is closer than ever to real power.
items.summary: Pastor Douglas Wilson, who influences Defense Secretary nominee Pete Hegseth, advocates for theocratic governance and restricting voting rights from women and nonbelievers. The investigation reveals Wilson's proximity to potential executive power through his mentorship relationship.
items.pullQuote: Wilson advocates restricting voting rights from women and nonbelievers

items.title: The Iran War Is Not a Just War
items.excerpt: Operation Epic Fury fails almost all the tests of righteous warfare.
items.summary: An analysis of hypothetical military action against Iran concludes that "Operation Epic Fury" fails traditional just war theory criteria. The piece evaluates proposed military strategies against established ethical frameworks for righteous warfare.
items.pullQuote: Proposed Iran military action fails just war theory tests

items.title: 💥 Stagflation Is Back. GDP Cut in Half. US Debt hit $38.9 Trillion. Oil Above $100. Here's What to Do.
items.excerpt: GDP crashed to 0.7%, oil topped $100, America's debt spiral hit $38.9 trillion, and recession odds jumped to 25%. Here's What It Means for You.
items.summary: U.S. GDP growth slowed dramatically to 0.7% while oil prices surged above $100 per barrel, creating stagflation conditions not seen since the 1970s. The national debt reached $38.9 trillion as recession probability estimates climbed to 25%, prompting concerns about simultaneous economic stagnation and inflation.
items.pullQuote: GDP crashed to 0.7% while oil topped $100 per barrel

briefing.editorsNote: Today's stories remind us that beneath surface headlines lie deeper truths requiring careful analysis and thoughtful preparation.

bullets.text: Critical thinking means questioning the premises everyone else accepts as obvious starting points.
Week 5 college baseball analytics reveal Vahn Lackey and Trey Beard as standout performers.
Obsidian skill graphs can transform AI agents into automated content teams for multiple outputs.
Adam Smith's 250-year-old economic principles still guide today's most pressing policy debates.
GDP crashed to 0.7% while oil hit $100, creating dangerous stagflation not seen since 1970s.
